metadata.title: Audit a Codex Session for the Five Supervisory Capacities
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B09: palette=claude but the outro is 'un-annotated' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. When a build goes wrong, it's always one of five named failures. Naming which capacity was absent makes the next build's discipline more deliberate.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: This is Liam, in for Bear. When a build goes wrong, it's always one of five named failures. Naming which capacity was absent makes the next build's discipline m

beats.narration_text: Five supervisory capacities: PA — Plausibility Auditing, noticing something is off. PF — Problem Formulation, deciding what to build. TO — Tool Orchestration, choosing Ask vs Code Mode. IJ — Interpretive Judgment, supplying meaning Codex can't infer. EI — Executive Integration, holding the build toward the long-range goal.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Five supervisory capacities: PA — Plausibility Auditing, noticing something is off. PF — Problem Formulation, deciding what to build. TO — Tool Orchestration, c

beats.narration_text: claude "Read this Codex session transcript and label the supervisory capacity exercised at each step: PA, PF, TO, IJ, EI, or 'none' for steps where only generation ran. For each labeled step, state a one-sentence reason for the label." < session_transcript.md

props.command: claude "Label each step in this session transcript with the supervisory capacity exercised: PA, PF, TO, IJ, EI, or 'none'. One-sentence reason per label." < session_transcript.md

build.suggested: suggested prompt: claude "Read this Codex session transcript and label the supervisory capacity exercised at each step: PA, PF, TO, IJ, EI, or 'none' for steps where only generat

beats.narration_text: capacity_auditor.py — reads session transcript, labels each step with capacity, produces annotated timeline, highlights gaps — steps that should have had supervisory work but had none.

props.code:
# reads session transcript and labels supervisory capacities
import capacity_engine, timeline_reporter

transcript = open('session_transcript.md').read()
labeled = capacity_engine.label(transcript)
gaps = capacity_engine.find_gaps(labeled)

timeline_reporter.print(labeled)
if gaps:
    print(f'\nGAPS FOUND at steps: {[g.step for g in gaps]}')
    for g in gaps:
        print(f'  Step {g.step}: {g.expected_capacity} expected, none exercised')

build.suggested: suggested prompt: capacity_auditor.py — reads session transcript, labels each step with capacity, produces annotated timeline, highlights gaps — steps that should have had superv

beats.narration_text: Horizontal timeline with 8 step nodes. Capacity labels animate in above each node: PF above step 1, TO above step 2, none — gray node — above step 3, PA above step 4, none above step 5, IJ above step 6, EI above step 7, none above step 8. After all labels appear, GAP annotations appear below the gray nodes at steps 3, 5, and 8.
Compare session where EI was absent — show the constraint violation traced back to the specific step.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Compare session where EI was absent — show the constraint violation traced back to the specific step.

beats.narration_text: Same timeline but step 7 node is gray — EI absent. An arrow extends from step 7 down to a violation box: CONSTRAINT VIOLATION at step 30, out-of-scope feature introduced. A dashed line connects the violation box back to step 7's gray node. Caption: EI gap at step 7 leads to constraint drift leads to 23-prompt correction cycle.

The five-capacity framework converts 'what went wrong?' from a vague retrospective into a structural diagnosis. EI absent at step 7 means no one was checking whether the build was still heading where it was supposed to go. The correction cycle at step 30 was not bad luck — it was predictable from the gap at step 7.
